title: Character Temperament
description: Evocative words describing a person's nature or temperament, each with associations to spark characterization.
entries: RUTHLESS — merciless, cold-eyed, spares no one
TENDER — gentle, soft-handed, easily moved
GUARDED — wary, closed-off, slow to trust
RECKLESS — heedless, headlong, courting ruin
DEVOUT — faithful, reverent, steady in belief
SLY — cunning, sidelong, quietly scheming
BRAZEN — bold, shameless, loud without apology
MEEK — mild, yielding, shrinking from notice
VAIN — preening, mirror-loving, hungry for praise
STEADFAST — unwavering, rooted, true to a promise
VOLATILE — combustible, quick-tempered, hard to predict
ALOOF — distant, detached, above the fray
GENEROUS — open-handed, giving, glad to share
BITTER — soured, resentful, nursing old wounds
CURIOUS — probing, wide-eyed, always asking
DOMINEERING — bossy, overbearing, needing control
WHIMSICAL — playful, fanciful, led by fancy
STOIC — enduring, unflinching, hides the ache
PETTY — small-minded, spiteful, keeps a score
GALLANT — chivalrous, brave, quick to defend
SNIDE — sneering, cutting, mocking under breath
EARNEST — sincere, heartfelt, means every word
GREEDY — grasping, hoarding, never quite full
SERENE — calm, unruffled, still at the core
IMPULSIVE — rash, spur-driven, acts before thought
LOYAL — devoted, dependable, stays through storms
CYNICAL — jaded, doubting, expecting the worst
FLAMBOYANT — showy, theatrical, dressed to dazzle
TIMID — nervous, hesitant, afraid to step out
SHREWD — sharp, calculating, reads a room fast
NAIVE — trusting, unworldly, easily fooled
PROUD — dignified, upright, will not stoop
MISCHIEVOUS — impish, teasing, up to no good
SOMBER — grave, downcast, weighed by seriousness
GRACIOUS — courteous, warm, puts others at ease
OBSTINATE — stubborn, mulish, will not budge
PASSIONATE — fervent, burning, all-in on the cause
COWARDLY — craven, flinching, first to flee
WITTY — quick-tongued, clever, sharp with a jest
SOLITARY — lone, self-kept, content apart
ZEALOUS — driven, ardent, fired by a mission
CALLOUS — hardened, unfeeling, deaf to the plea
HUMBLE — modest, unassuming, shuns the spotlight
MERCURIAL — changeable, moody, shifting like weather
NOBLE — high-minded, honorable, holds to a code
